candidates.why: Primary top-line revenue measure; clearly labeled and matches target definition for total revenue/sales.
Financial income, not operating revenue; excluded because REVT should reflect operating revenue.

Represents net operating result, not top-line revenue; excluded for REVT.

Tax line, not revenue; excluded.

candidates.why: Net income, not revenue; excluded.

candidates.why: Balancing item around net income; not revenue.

Pretax income, not revenue; excluded.

Balancing item, not revenue; excluded.

variables.notes: ‘Total revenue’ is the clear top-line revenue measure; other candidates are finance income, taxes, or profit subtotals and are therefore excluded.

candidates.why: Represents direct material costs; a classic component of cost of goods sold and directly tied to production costs.
Explicit COGS line; likely used in some years instead of or in addition to ‘Direct materials’.
Broad personnel costs; appears more like operating overhead than strictly COGS, so excluded for pure COGS mapping.
Component of personnel expenses; generally SG&A/overhead and not strictly COGS in this context.

candidates.row_label: Stock-Based Compensation in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: Specifically SG&A-related SBC, not direct production cost; excluded from COGS.
Likely SG&A/overhead rather than direct production; excluded from COGS.
Marketing expense; SG&A, not COGS.
Overhead expense; SG&A, not direct COGS.
Net operating subtotal, not a COGS component; excluded.
Financing costs, not production costs; excluded.
Financial, not production; excluded.
Financial, not production; excluded.
Net finance result; not COGS.
Inventory component on balance sheet, not an income statement expense line; not used for COGS mapping.

variables.notes: Both ‘Direct materials’ and ‘Cost of goods sold’ are direct production costs; using both as a priority list allows for label changes across years without double-counting.

candidates.why: Operating income line (and negative here), not an expense; excluded from SG&A components.
Broad personnel costs likely representing SG&A-type overhead; good SG&A component.
Subcomponent of personnel expenses; a classic SG&A expense.

candidates.row_label: Stock-Based Compensation in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: Explicit SG&A stock-based compensation; clearly within SG&A definition.
Likely includes various SG&A/overhead costs (advertising, rental, bad debts, etc.); appropriate SG&A bucket.

final_choice.row_label: Stock-Based Compensation in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses

variables.notes: Selected multiple overhead expense components (personnel-related and other operating expenses) and excluded ‘Other Operating Income’ per rule to avoid including income or totals.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Capitalized Research & Development Expenses - Supplemental
candidates.why: Amortization of previously capitalized R&D, which is a depreciation/amortization item, not current-period R&D expense.

variables.notes: No explicit current-period R&D expense line appears in the statements; the only candidate is amortization of capitalized R&D, which is a D&A item, so XRD is treated as missing (0) per instructions.

candidates.why: Explicit interest expense component; best available direct measure of interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Interest on lease liabilities; another clear interest expense line.
Represents profit/loss attributable to NCI, not interest expense; excluded.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease interest; suitable as a fallback interest expense series.
Equity component, not interest; excluded.
Equity component, not interest; excluded.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: Maps XINT to explicit interest expense lines only; uses a priority list so the first non-missing interest expense value per year is taken, excluding any non-controlling interest equity lines.

candidates.row_label: Equity holders of the parent company
candidates.why: Income statement attribution of profit, not a balance sheet equity total; excluded for BE.
Component of equity but not the total equity attributable to owners; a subtotal, so not used for BE.
Component line(s) of equity, not the full owners’ equity total; excluded to avoid partial measures.

candidates.row_label: Total equity attributable to owners of parent
candidates.why: Directly matches desired BE definition (equity attributable to shareholders of parent), excluding non-controlling interests.
Includes both owners’ equity and non-controlling interests; only a fallback if owners’ equity line were absent, which it is not.

final_choice.row_label: Total equity attributable to owners of parent

variables.notes: Uses ‘Total equity attributable to owners of parent’ to capture book equity excluding non-controlling interests, consistent with BE+MIB construction.

candidates.why: Income statement share of profit for NCI, not the balance sheet interest; BE/MIB require balance sheet amounts, so this is not chosen.
Balance sheet line for NCI; suitable MIB candidate but duplicated by plural form.
Balance sheet NCI line; directly matches MIB definition and preferred due to standard wording.
Income statement minority interest line; not the balance sheet NCI amount; excluded.

variables.notes: Selected the balance sheet ‘Non-controlling interests’ line as MIB to complement BE, ensuring BE excludes NCI and MIB captures it separately.
